violations.text: Establishment's Rhode Island Certified Food Safety Manager license is expired. FMC to re-instate. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026
The hand-washing sink in the bar area was blocked by folded boxes. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Raw shelled eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as soups and beans in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Stuffies and seafood stuffing were observed held at 98 and 119 degrees Fahrenheit on top of oven. Per staff, items were placed on top of oven recently. Establishment to cease this practice. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and placed into walk-in cooler to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

The following were observed in poor repair: -doors at 2 door prep unit (insulation exposed) -gaskets at 2 door prep unit -battery nonfunctional for right side hand sink in men's restroom New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine and fanguards in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of pink residue, dust and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026
The hand-washing sink in the bar area was blocked by folded boxes. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Stuffies were observed held at 71 degrees Fahrenheit on top of oven. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and placed into freezer to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

A take-out container with lemons was being stored in the ice used for food. Food shall be protected from contamination. Container was removed from ice and ice was disposed during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/14/2023

Steak sauce, pasta, and chop-suey, located in the walk-in unit were observed marked with a date that exceeded the 7 days limit for ready to eat foods. Food items were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/14/2023

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/14/2023
